rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAY FUNG MEI TAM TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Hearing required. Having reviewed and weighed the admissible evidence presented in connection with this motion, the Court concludes that Petioner has not carried his burden of showing by a preponderance of the evidence that he wlll obtain a favorable judgment as to his lack of capacity, undue influence, or elder abuse claims. The remaining issue whether Petitioner has rights as an omitted child requires discussion. Has Petitioner carried his burden of proving a legal adoption under Chinese law given the competing expert opinions? Assuming he has carried that burden, is Probate Code section 21622 the relevant statute (given its specific language requiring ignorance of "birth", which seems to speak more to the situation of a father not knowing of his child's existence) or, alternatively, are sections 21620 and 21621(a) the statutes that govern such a situation of an adopted child? If section 21622 applies, has Petitioner carried his burden of proving that at the time she created her estate plan decedent was ignorant of his "birth" in a relevant way--i.e., does the statute include a decedent who had forgotten about an adoption?
